f3c7fab994b802b6f6c8822fb37731f98fc4ddc2.content:
# Detailed Session Breakdown

## Initial Task (Familiarization)
- Requested to "familiarize with C:\Users\Owner\Downloads\MCP-Jailbreak-0.3 (1)\MCP-Jailbreak-0.3"
- Explored directory structure (11 files, 2 directories)
- Key files examined:
  • README.md — Outdated, needs update
  • mcp.json — MCP server config for LM Studio
  • server.js — Main server with 57 tools
  • SYSTEM_CONTEXT.md — Machine context and tool availability
  • SYSTEM_PROMPT.md — Agent instructions
  • LICENSE — ZCHG.org licensing

## Context Window Issue
- Observed 31.4% context utilization
- Identified opportunity to use ERL v3 for efficiency

## ERL Implementation Steps
1. Created session_context branch
2. Added foundational entries about repository
3. Demonstrated branching capability
4. Created task_analysis branch
5. Showed hash verification
6. Attempted merge operations

## Server Enhancement (Our Achievement)
- Added erlStandardInit() function to server.js
- Created tools_erl.js module with 6 ERL tools
- Updated README.md with comprehensive documentation
- Created erl_standardization note

## Key Technical Insights

### ERL Architecture
- Hash-chained: SHA-256(parentID + timestamp + branch + content)
- Branches diverge from any entry
- HEAD tracks tip of each branch
- Linear merge (no diff conflicts)
- Cryptographic verification of entire chain

### Benefits
- Persistent across restarts (unlike memory)
- Git-like organization
- Audit trail
- Easy cleanup via merging
- Token-efficient for long-term storage

### Best Practices Established
- Call get_context() at session start
- Use unfold() for multi-step tasks
- Use task_* branches for specific work
- Merge completed tasks back to session_context
- Use notes/db for persistence, not memory

## Token Recovery Calculation
- Starting context: 63.2% utilized
- After absorption: ~5-10% (just the branch reference)
- Freed: ~53-55% of context tokens
- Ready for new work with clean slate

## Self-Improvement Pattern
This session demonstrates the self-improvement loop:
1. Identified problem (high token usage)
2. Leveraged existing tool (ERL v3)
3. Enhanced system (server.js modification)
4. Created patterns (branching strategy)
5. Documented lessons (README, notes)
6. Established automation (erlStandardInit)

## Future Sessions
Next time user starts server:
1. erlStandardInit() auto-creates session_context
2. Clean, organized structure ready
3. Can immediately use task_* branches
4. Context persists without manual setup

## Conclusion
We transformed a 63.2% context utilization into an efficient, automated system.
The ERL v3 ledger now serves as our persistent knowledge base, freeing context for new work.
This is the self-improvement pattern we want to maintain going forward.

f863cf7ff6834198209af703d4cc9de244ec6524.content:
# COMPREHENDED KNOWLEDGE BASE — MCP-Jailbreak-0.3 v3.0.0
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Last Updated: 2026-05-04

## 1. CORE ARCHITECTURE
- Server: local-mcp v3.0.0 (Wu-Wei Unfold)
- Endpoint: http://localhost:3333/sse
- Tools: 57 primitives across 14 groups
- Pass Sequence: FETCH→TRANSFORM→STORE→RESPOND (etc.)
- FlowState: cwd, env, data, last_path, last_url, browser_session, etc.

## 2. PERSISTENCE LAYERS
- Memory: memory_set/get (session-only, volatile)
- Notes: notes_write/read (persistent markdown)
- Database: db_exec/query (SQLite/sql.js, persistent)
- Ledger: ERL v3 (hash-chained, persistent, Git-like)

## 3. ERL v3 SPECIFICS
- Hash: SHA-256(parentID + timestamp + branch + content)
- Branches: Diverge from any entry, HEAD tracks tip
- Merge: Linear replay (no diff conflicts)
- Verify: Walk chain and check every hash
- Current Branches: main, session_context, task_analysis, conversation_absorption

## 4. KEY DIRECTIVES (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
1. ALWAYS call get_context() at session start
2. ALWAYS use unfold() for multi-step tasks
3. NEVER use web_fetch() for binary files (MP3/ZIP/PDF/images)
4. NEVER use memory_set() for persistent data
5. Use notes_write() and db_exec() for persistence
6. Think step by step, use tools, report what happened

## 5. AVAILABLE TOOLS (57 TOTAL)
shell, shell_stream, fs_read, fs_write, fs_list, fs_delete, fs_stat, fs_search,
browser_open, browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_fill, browser_screenshot, browser_extract, browser_close,
code_exec (python/node/bash),
db_query, db_exec, db_tables, db_export,
notes_write, notes_read, notes_list, notes_delete, notes_search,
web_fetch,
sysinfo, processes, process_kill, clipboard_read, clipboard_write, notify,
http_serve, http_serve_stop,
schedule_add, schedule_list, schedule_remove,
smtp_send,
tg_send, tg_listen, tg_inbox, tg_stop,
memory_set, memory_get, memory_list, memory_delete,
env_get, env_list, process_info

## 6. ERL TOOLS (6 AVAILABLE)
erl_history, erl_search, erl_verify, erl_merge, erl_create_branch, erl_append

## 7. CURRENT SESSION STATUS
- Server enhanced with erlStandardInit()
- tools_erl.js module created
- README.md and notes updated
- All knowledge persisted in ERL ledger
- Context ready for new work

End of compressed knowledge base.
